zh.Bethersden, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特貝黑斯達
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace於1610年1月出生在英國英格蘭肯特貝黑斯達時，她的父親Sir William Lovelace V26歲，母親Anne Barne22歲。她於1628年9月25日與Rev. John Gorsuch在英國英格蘭賀特福爾格亞特 維蒙德利結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1652年6月2日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州享年42歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace于1610年1月出生在英国英格兰肯特貝黑斯達时，她的父亲Sir William Lovelace V26岁，母亲Anne Barne22岁。她于1628年9月25日与Rev. John Gorsuch在英国英格兰賀特福爾格亞特 維蒙德利结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1652年6月2日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州享年42岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。

nameMeanings.name: Lovelace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Loveless  . The spelling is apparently theresult of folk etymology, which understood the word as a nickname fora dandy fond of lace. The modern sense of this word is, however, notattested until the 16th century and at the time of surname formationit meant only ‘cord’ or ‘shoelace’.

variants.name: Loveless
meanings: nickname from Middle English loveles ‘loveless, without love’, probably in the sense ‘fancy free’.
some early examples, such as Richard Lovelas (Kent 1344), may have as their second element Middle English las(se) ‘girl’, ‘maiden’.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

nameMeanings.name: Barnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Barnaby   or its learned equivalent   Barnabas .

variants.name: Barnabas
meanings: From the New Testament, where Barnabas represents a Greek form of an Aramaic name meaning ‘son of consolation’. St Barnabas was a Jewish Cypriot, one of the earliest Christian missionaries, who sold his property and gave the proceeds to the Church. He worked with St Paul until about ad 48 .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace was born in January 1610, in Bethersden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William Lovelace V, was 26 and her mother, Anne Barne, was 22. She married Rev. John Gorsuch on 25 September 1628, in Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 2 June 1652, in Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 42, and was buried in Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace est née en janvier 1610, à Bethersden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Lovelace V, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Anne Barne, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Rev. John Gorsuch le 25 septembre 1628, à Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 juin 1652, à Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterrée à Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace im Januar 1610 in Bethersden, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Lovelace V 26 und ihre Mutter Anne Barne 22. Sie hat am 25. September 1628 in Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom Rev. John Gorsuch geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 2. Juni 1652 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Virginia, British Colonial America und wurde in Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace è nata il nel gennaio 1610 nel Bethersden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir William Lovelace V, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Anne Barne, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Rev. John Gorsuch il 25 settembre 1628, nel Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 2 giugno 1652, nel Virginia, British Colonial America, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolta a Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Barnes Lovelaceが1610年1月にイギリスイギリスケントベスエルスデンで生まれた時，父Sir William Lovelace Vは26歳，母Anne Barneは22歳であった。1628年9月25日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアグレアト ウイモンドリーでRev. John Gorsuchと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1652年6月2日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州で42歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州にて埋葬された。

ko.London St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 앤드루 비 테 워드로브 위스 스트 아 블랙프리아르스
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace(이)가 1610년 1월 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 베더즈든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Lovelace V(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Barne(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1628년 9월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 그리트 윈이몽리에서 Rev. John Gorsuch(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1652년 6월 2일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace nasceu em janeiro de 1610, em Bethersden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Lovelace V, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Anne Barne, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Rev. John Gorsuch em 25 de setembro de 1628, em Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 2 de junho de 1652, em Virginia, British Colonial America, com 42 anos, e foi sepultada em Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.London St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St: ст андрю би т вардроб вит ст анн блэккфрярс, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace родилась январь 1610 г. в местности бетирсден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir William Lovelace V было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Barne, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Rev. John Gorsuch 25 сентября 1628 г. в местности грит вимондли, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 2 июня 1652 г. в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 42 лет и была похоронена в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Anne Barnes Lovelace nació en enero de 1610, en Bethersden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Lovelace V, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Anne Barne, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Rev. John Gorsuch el 25 de septiembre de 1628, en Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 2 de junio de 1652, en Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultada en Virginia, British Colonial America.
